data.content:
I'm building the Blazor Web App with **Auto render mode** (Server for fast first paint, then WebAssembly for snappy client-side interaction). This includes:

- **Auto interactivity** — first visit renders via Server (fast), subsequent interactions run in WebAssembly (no round-trips)
- **Individual accounts** authentication via ASP.NET Identity
- **Dispatcher dashboard** — filterable shipment list with inline status updates
- **Customer tracking page** — search by tracking number, view status + ETA with progress bar
- **New Shipment form** — dispatchers create shipments
- **API endpoints** — minimal APIs backing the WASM client
- **Seed data** — 5 sample shipments for demo

Working on it now — I'll verify it builds cleanly.

**Key architecture decisions:**
| Requirement | Solution |
|---|---|
| Fast first load | Auto mode → Server renders first paint |
| No server round-trips after load | WebAssembly takes over interaction |
| Interactive controls on every page | `@rendermode InteractiveAuto` on all pages |
| Authentication | ASP.NET Identity with Individual accounts |
| Worldwide latency | WASM runs locally in-browser after download |

Run with `dotnet run --project ShipmentTracker/ShipmentTracker` — 5 sample shipments are seeded automatically.
